stories.title: tools/curate-memory.sh: dedup + decay + archive
acceptanceCriteria: tools/curate-memory.sh exists and is executable
Script deduplicates learnings.jsonl by key: only the latest entry per key is kept
Script applies confidence decay: entries older than 90 days get confidence -= 2, older than 180 days get confidence -= 4
Script archives entries with confidence < 3 to .multica/learnings-archive.jsonl
All writes are atomic (mktemp + mv)
bash -n tools/curate-memory.sh passes
shellcheck tools/curate-memory.sh reports no error-level findings
Script is idempotent: running twice produces the same result

stories.title: stop.sh: git commit learnings + notepad prune on completion
acceptanceCriteria: hooks/stop.sh on DONE path: git commits .multica/learnings.jsonl when it exists and has uncommitted changes
hooks/stop.sh on DONE path: prunes Working Memory entries older than 7 days from .multica/notepad.md
git commit uses message 'chore(knowledge): update learnings [skip ci]'
All git operations are guarded (fail silently if not a git repo or git unavailable)
Notepad prune preserves Priority Context and Manual Notes sections unchanged
bash -n hooks/stop.sh passes
shellcheck hooks/stop.sh reports no error-level findings
stop.sh still exits 0 on DONE path and exit 2 on loop-active path

stories.title: session-start.sh: stat-based staleness detection for learnings
acceptanceCriteria: hooks/session-start.sh checks source files listed in each learning's 'files' field
When a source file does not exist: marks learning as '[possibly stale]' in injected context
When a source file mtime is newer than learning 'ts': marks learning as '[possibly stale]'
Stale detection only runs on entries that have non-empty 'files' field
Non-stale entries are injected normally without any staleness marker
bash -n hooks/session-start.sh passes
shellcheck hooks/session-start.sh reports no error-level findings

stories.title: stop.sh: memory consolidation prompt on completion
acceptanceCriteria: hooks/stop.sh on DONE path: writes a memory consolidation prompt to .multica/state/consolidation-prompt.txt
Prompt instructs a haiku subagent to: read recent issue comments, extract valuable learnings, append to .multica/learnings.jsonl in correct JSONL format
Prompt includes the format spec: {ts, skill, type, key, insight, confidence, source, branch, commit, files}
Prompt instructs: only write high-confidence (>=7) learnings, skip uncertain ones
File is written only when MULTICA_ISSUE_ID is set (issue context exists)
stop.sh still exits correctly after writing the prompt file

stories.title: smoke: v0.4.0 knowledge management scenarios
acceptanceCriteria: tests/smoke/run-claude.sh contains Scenario 8: curate-memory.sh dedup test (two entries with same key, verify only latest remains after curate)
tests/smoke/run-claude.sh contains Scenario 9: staleness detection (fixture learning with non-existent source file, verify '[possibly stale]' appears in session-start output)
tests/smoke/run-claude.sh contains Scenario 10: notepad prune (fixture notepad with 8-day-old Working Memory entry, run stop.sh DONE path, verify old entry removed)
bash tests/smoke/run-claude.sh exits 0 with FAIL: 0 (all scenarios including 1-10)
